title: Oxalis neorufescens
taxonomy: Oxalis neorufescens is classified within the family Oxalidaceae, which consists of the woodsorrel or oxalis family. This family is part of the larger order Oxalidales and is native to South America. The genus Oxalis is a diverse group of flowering plants, often referred to as wood sorrel or sour grass. Oxalis neorufescens is one of the species within this genus, characterized by its unique features and distribution. The classification of Oxalis neorufescens reflects its evolutionary history and relationship to other plants within the Oxalidaceae family.

intro: Oxalis neorufescens is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae), commonly known as the purple wood sorrel. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Oxalis neorufescens include oxalic acid, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its medicinal and color properties.
